observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, through the GSO-Designate to implement the following measures:
Maintain property cards per class of PPE, and not per project or disbursement; and
Provide a unique property number for each item.
We further recommended and Management agreed, through the members of the Inventory Committee to undertake the following:
Ensure that the physical inventory count be fully conducted by all the members of said committee;
Revise the RPCPPE by not repeatedly showing the same property;
Indicate the number of unit and not the cost of PPE item under the columns for quantity per property card and quantity per physical count; and
Fill-in the “Property Number” and “Remarks” columns of the RPCPPE provided under Annex 37, Volume 2 of the NGAS Manual.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize impairment loss pending disposal of the unserviceable properties to fairly present the PPE account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management consider the option of executing a document to formalize the transfer of not just the control but also the ownership of these properties.
Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to derecognize these assets from the Municipality's books, and provide copies of the JEV and the MOA/DOD to the respective beneficiaries and the Audit Team to ensure proper recording in their respective accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Assessor, the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to collaborate in the review of transactions concerning Land account and reconcile their records to ascertain the correctness of Land account balance.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to commence an action for the titling of these lots to ensure ownership and fully protect the interest of the Municipality from third party claims.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to prepare and maintain the SLCs for each type of inventory following the prescribed format and requirements outlined in Section 120, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, to facilitate efficient reconciliation and timely detection of errors or discrepancies affecting the account.
We further recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Inventory Committee to strictly adhere to the regular physical count of all inventories and ensure that the RPCI is duly certified and submitted to the Audit Team no later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
Lastly, we recommended and the Management agreed direct the concerned offices to reconcile the balances shown in the financial statements and in the RPCI and for the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to fairly present the Inventories account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, agreed to immediately update their records and submit to the Municipal Accountant the accurate computation of RPT/SET receivables supported with the reconciled data of assessed values from the Assessor’s Office for recording of adjustments in the books of the LGU.
We further recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to maintain subsidiary ledgers for RPT and SET receivables, reconcile regularly the accounts with the Municipal Treasurer’s records, and consider the following proposed adjusting entries to correct the overstated balances of receivable accounts in the financial statements:
General Fund:
Special Education Fund:

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Agriculturist, to:
retrieve all existing records of the dispersal project;
provide convincing justification why the existence of the Breeding Stocks can no longer be established; and
initiate or file a request for relief from property accountability.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to prepare the required schedules/subsidiary ledgers of the above-mentioned accounts to enable the Audit Team to confirm the validity of the account balances pursuant to Section 70, Volume I, of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the LDRRM Officer, submit a written justification for the non-implementation of the PPAs under the 70 per cent Mitigation Fund, despite having an approved budget and investment plan.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in collaboration with involved departments and offices, develop clear strategies to ensure full implementation of the proposed disaster-related PPAs in the LDRRMFIP aimed at strengthening the Municipality’s capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MBO, the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Officer to reconcile their records and for the latter to submit a revised LDRRMIP, if necessary.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the LDRRM Officer, agreed to ensure that appropriations from supplemental budgets and the correct amount of STF are incorporated in the LDRRMF investment plan.
Further, we recommended that the LDRRM Officer verify the year-end balance of STF from the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the correct balance is used in the formulation of the AIP.
Lasty, we recommended that the LDRRM Officer, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant, clearly establish the amounts pertaining to the MOOE and Capital Outlay and conduct regular reconciliation with the balances to present accurate and verifiable amounts of LDRRMF related accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management seek the concurrence of the Commission on Audit and secure a clearance from the Solicitor General prior to the hiring of a private lawyer whose functions are not merely confine to purely technical matters but includes the rendering of legal services.

We recommended and Management, through the HRMO, agreed to refrain from hiring consultant whose functions can be performed by existing workforce of the LGU to avoid the unnecessary duplication of functions and wastage of government funds.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, through the General Services Officer, agreed to prioritize the registration, transfer, and titling of these parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Pinamungajan to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386. In alternative, we recommended for the creation of Land Titling Committee to intensify/expedite the titling efforts of the municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to provide a clear and satisfactory explanation for the above-noted excessive cost. Moving forward, we recommended strict adherence to engineering standards and guidelines when preparing cost estimates.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant and other concerned departments, agreed to submit the lacking documents of the above-mentioned fund transfers together with the status of implementation and fund utilization report from the respective Barangays for evaluation by the Audit Team. Henceforth, ensure that subsequent fund transfers are supported with complete documentation to ensure propriety and accountability of government funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to address the issue on manpower constraints faced by the Accounting Unit, and thereafter, ensure that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the prescribed timeline under Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 on the submission of the Municipality’s BRS to enable timely verification and communication of audit results, particularly when deficiencies are identified.

We recommended and the Management, through the Head of IAS, agreed to create an Internal Audit Charter and develop an Internal Audit Plan which are both essential in ensuring that the IAS operates with clarity, authority, and focus, contributing to effective governance, risk management, and internal controls within the Municipality.
We further recommended which the IAS agreed to fully adopt the IASPPS and ICSPPS in the performance of their functions, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2018-003 dated November 21, 2018.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to avoid assigning the Head of the IAS responsibilities for non-audit operational activities which may impair the independence and objectivity of her internal audit functions.
Further, we recommended which the Head of the IAS agreed to assess the potential impact on her independence and/or objectivity before accepting any roles and responsibilities outside of internal auditing.

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal GAD Focal Person, agreed to submit a written explanation regarding the implementation of PPAs that may not be aligned or related to the promotion of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
We also recommended to ensure that all GAD-related programs, projects and activities are efficiently and judiciously assess to make them consistent with the provisions of R.A. No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMCs Nos. 2013-1 and 2014-1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Inventory Team to conduct an actual and complete physical count
of PPE accounts under General and Special Education Fund, render a report
thereon and provide a copy to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124,
Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs;
• enjoin the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Officer to strictly follow the
format provided in Annex 49, Volume II of the same Manual in the
preparation of RPCPPE and maintain the required Property Cards; and
• ensure that the ARE or PAR are renewed every after three years or if there
is a change in custodianship/accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to adopt the prescribed accounting
rules and regulations when it comes to recording of inventory items,
particularly the perpetual inventory method, as required on the above cited
provisions.

We recommended and Management agreed to closely monitor the submission of
financial reports and documents within the prescribed period to facilitate the
timely audit of the LGU’s transactions.

We also recommended to institute appropriate actions against the accountable
officials if necessary, so that this practice may be avoided in the future.

We recommended and Management agreed to assign a BAC Secretariat
member who shall focus on advertising and/or posting of the bidding
opportunities. Further, we recommended and further agreed by the
Management to direct the BAC Secretariat in posting the complete bidding
documents, minutes of pre-bid conferences, Notice of Award and the NTP and
the related approved contract at the PhilGEPs and Municipality’s website, and
attach proof of posting thereof to the DV as one of the supporting documents.

We recommended that Management:
• exhaust all possible remedies for collection/settlement of the outstanding
receivable accounts provided in Annex-B;
• record the liquidation of Intelligence Funds (IF) with transmittal documents
duly acknowledged by ICFAU and submit the JEV to the COA ATL in
accordance with Item 6.3.3 (e) of JMC no. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015; and
• direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular monitoring and periodic
analysis of all the receivables to ensure that these are being collected when
these become due and demandable pursuant to existing COA rules and
regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a. Direct the personnel-in-charge to submit the Consolidated Monthly Report
of Fuel Consumption and Consolidated Monthly Report of Official Travels
in CY 2021 to the Audit Team to facilitate the validation of the
reasonableness of fuel consumption of the LGU;
b. Direct the Accountant to examine carefully the completeness of the
supporting documents for every transaction to ensure that all
disbursements are properly documented; and
c. Direct all Department Heads and Drivers to ensure that Driver’s Trip
Tickets are properly accomplished and that all the necessary information
indicated in the forms are all filled-out for every trip undertaken.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit an explanation on the
excessive unit cost of the work items in the contract cost for the Audit Team’s
validation, and henceforth, be more careful in the preparation of cost estimates.

We recommended and Management agreed through the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to stop the practice of splitting the purchase requisitions or
purchase orders/contracts and strictly adhere with the applicable provisions of
the RIRR of RA 9184.

Further, the Management committed to submit the required explanation on the
splitting of requisition and purchase order for the above items.

We recommended that Management submit the required documents on the
purchase of monthly cell card load to Audit Team, for validation, to avoid the
issuance of suspension or possible disallowance. Further, we recommended that
all the officers involved in the processing of claims must ensure at all times that
the supporting papers are complete and proper before payment.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit the LGU’s monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF, certified correct by the
Municipal Accountant in prescribed form on or before the 15th day after the end
of each month.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 8 of RA
11313 and Section 9 of its IRR to ensure enforcement of the provisions of the
Safe Spaces Act.

observations.managementAction: The documents were already submitted to the Audit Team for the said list of projects from February 10 to March 9, 2021.
observations.reason: The contracts and all the supporting documents were submitted.

observations.managementAction: 2020 paid liquidated damages for the completion of said projects. These were all forwarded to the Regional Technical Services Office for contract review.
observations.reason: Copies of liquidated damages were presented to the Audit Team for verification.

observations.managementAction:
AAR 2020 Supporting documents (leave form) for the salary of Marlon Kyamko was attached during the submission of management’s comment in CY 2020 AOM as well as the copy of the cancelled voucher in relation to Tri-J Marketing.
The distribution list for the recipients of rice was also submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Substantially complied with the submission of the required documents.

observations.managementAction: The BAC secretary posted the print screen shots of their postings in the GPPB Online Portal.
observations.reason: The BAC was already able to present the print screen shots of their postings in the GPPB Online Portal.

observations.managementAction: The Management did not collect any liquidated damages on the said contractors on the ground that the contractors claimed that the delayed completion of said projects were due to the number of days extended in the completion of said projects.
observations.reason: Considered as implemented since it was shown on the ground that the late completion of the projects were completed.

observations.managementAction: The Management was expecting that the delayed completion of the contractors will suffice on full compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Explanations submitted on the number of days extended in the completion of said projects were considered by the Audit Team as full compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages were already collected last December 2020.
observations.reason: Complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.managementAction: The total amount in Construction in Progress was closed to Other Construction in Progress account.
observations.reason: The amounts in Construction in Progress were transferred to its specific PPE account.

observations.managementAction: The biggest bulk of amount of receivables reflected in the AOM as outstanding and non-moving were the Intelligence Fund granted to Mayor Baricuatro amounting to ₱1,129,589.51 and Mayor Yapha accountable officers were also presented in the Report on unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Follow-up at COA Central Office will be conducted in the current year to determine the status of liquidation made by the LGU. Partial payments were made. Other payors already made partial payments for CY 2021 as reflected in the Report of Receivables reported quarterly to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: Justification on the granting of eligibilities to the recipients of SAP on persons living alone was sent by our Municipal Social Worker and Development Officer on the ground that it was not mentioned on the basis of the grant of SAP 1 but other ineligible beneficiaries already paid their obligation partially as reflected in our books of accounts and such collections were already remitted to DSWD Regional Office.
observations.reason: Copies of Official Receipts for those who were able to pay as well as the issued demand letters of the Management were submitted and verified by the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to the recipients and the agency was able to collect ₱17,000.00 upon receipt of the AOM. Some recipients were already dead but upon query to the auditors, this will now be shouldered to their respective estate.
observations.reason: The Management is still on the process of enforcing the collection of said receivables.

observations.managementAction: Of the total appropriation for the 20% Development Fund for CY 2018, only ₱1,200,000.00 remained unimplemented.
observations.reason: Actions were now undertaken to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund.

observations.managementAction: Partial payments were made. As of December 31, 2021, the remaining balance in the books of the LGU amounted to ₱1,950,000.00.
observations.reason: Demand letters will still be issued this year. Partial amounts were collected.

observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries were made as shown in the submitted JEV No. 401-140704 in CY 2014.
observations.reason: Effected already the adjusting entries but the entire amount was still not fully collected/liquidated.

observations.managementAction: Partial remittance was made in April 2021 to the Provincial Government amounting to ₱795,503.39.
observations.reason: The recommendation was still not fully complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the statutory positions of LDRRM Officer and LDRRM Staff responsible for Operations and Warnings be filled up immediately by the municipality, instead of designating existing employees to the same and in order to properly discharge the functions and responsibilities of the LDRRM Office in accordance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The municipality hired the LDRRMO III SG-18 in January, 2020.
observations.reason: During the Exit Conference, it was found out that the statutory positions were not completely filled-up. One position is vacant for no one meets the qualifications required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Development Council optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure that projects are implemented fully within the time frame. For projects that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts should be made to fast track the completion of the same and ensure close monitoring of its implementation in accordance with the Municipal Development Plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Out of the 41 projects of the LGU, only 26 were implemented and 15 were not implemented. Some of the projects are still on going as of December, 2019. The allocated amount which was not implemented during the year totaled ₱15,866,570.80.
observations.reason: Out of the 41 projects of the LGU, only 26 were implemented and 15 were not implemented. The allocated amount which was not implemented during the year totaled ₱15,866,570.80.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant will compute and record immediately the depreciation in the books
of accounts in order to present fairly the value of the affected accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Engineering
Department of the LGU will conduct further inspection of the projects to
determine the status of its completion.

We recommended and Management agreed to derecognize as Furniture,
Fixtures and Equipment those items costing below ₱10,000.00 and the
necessary adjustments will be effected in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Property Officer to
conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment recorded in Special
Education Fund; indicate the titles of the books comprising Library Book and
specify the snorkeling equipment constituting Marine and Fishery Equipment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Unit
and Treasury will now conduct periodic reconciliation of PPE to ensure that all
discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) require the GSO and
other custodians of supplies to undertake the actual physical inventory every
semester and to submit inventory reports to the Auditor; and (b) enjoin all stock
custodians and the Accounting Office to perform the reconciliation of the
inventories with the recorded balances in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to see to it that the conduct of
detailed engineering in various infrastructure projects shall at all times be
carried out in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and its 2016 Revised
IRR.

We also recommended that the municipal engineer immediately comply
with the requirements of the TITS office in order to facilitate contract review
and for the audit team to assess the propriety and reasonableness of the amount
paid.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit justifications
relative to the contractors’ failure to accomplish the said projects on time. We
also recommended to strictly enforce the terms and conditions provided for each
contract agreement. Lastly, we recommended to provide corresponding actions
in the non-completion of the mentioned projects such as but not limited to the
forfeiture of performance security, payment of liquidated damages or even
termination of the Contract as provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer
and other officials concerned be made to explain their failure to monitor the
completion of the projects; and liquidated damages be computed, imposed and
collected on the payment for the delivery beyond the expiry date.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer
and other officials concerned be made to explain their failure to monitor the
completion of the projects; and liquidated damages be computed, imposed and
collected on the payment for the delivery beyond the expiry date.

We recommended and Management agreed to still exhaust all possible
remedies for collection/settlement. Final demand letters must also be sent
immediately to the accountable officers in preparation for any legal action that
may be taken up against them.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct regular
monitoring and periodic analysis of all the receivables to ensure that these are
collected when these become due and demandable in pursuant to existing COA
rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to assess its existing
organizational structure so that a reasonable number of plantilla personnel will
be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive number of job-order.
Also, we recommended the Management to strictly adhere to the policies and
guidelines in the hiring of personnel under Contract of Services/Job-Order as
provided in CSC Memorandum Circular Nos. 17 & 24 s. 2002.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the members of the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) shall submit explanation/justification to
provide proof that the government was not prejudiced in any way in order to
avoid disallowance.

We also recommended that Management and the BAC members observe
the basic law on procurement in their future procurement activities.

Lastly, we recommended that the lacking documents be submitted by
Management to the Auditors for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accounting Unit of the municipality to compute and record immediately the depreciation in the books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of Infrastructure Assets and other affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Allowance for depreciation for Infrastructure Assets is already implemented in CY 2019. The agency submitted a Schedule of Depreciation which provides that the total Accumulated Depreciation for Infrastructure Assets is ₱8,440,180.94 as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health Officer to immediately comply and install the Automated Hematology Analyzer to facilitate the inspection by the COA TITS office and for the audit team to ascertain the reasonableness of the amount paid to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Automated hematology analyzer was already installed and being used by the Rural Health Office in CY 2019 after the completion of the building improvement and for the safety of the same.
observations.reason: Hematology Analyzer has already been installed and found to be well-functioning upon personal validation of the audit team during one of their travels in the municipality sometime in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare and timely submit the LGU’s Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant in prescribed form on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA Auditor of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Reports were submitted to COA were regularly emailed to the team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the GAD Accomplishment Report duly reviewed by the DILG with all the required attachments enumerated in Section 7 of the JMC to enable us to properly evaluate whether the planned programs/projects/activities and the GAD reported utilizations were all gender responsive and addresses the gender issues of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The GAD accomplishment report for CY 2018 and The GAD Accomplishment Report for the current year was received by the GAD Accounting Office end of February 2019 for the purpose of posting it in the DILG Portal.
observations.reason: The GAD Accomplishment Report for the current year was submitted along with the other financial reports for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Accounting Unit and Treasury conduct periodic reconciliation of PPE to ensure that all discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled. Also, we recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries in adjusting the duly investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Adjusting entry was made in March, 2019 per JEV No. 101-1903-036.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and to take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The discrepancy was due to the unrecorded official receipt of the transfer from Trust Fund account on the taxes withheld on government money payments.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2019, both accounts equally totaled ₱6,595,900.94.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant effect directly the necessary correction in the financial statement where it occurred following the requirements provided in PPSAS 3 including such disclosure of certain items in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Prior period adjustments are still incorporated in the financial statements but was presented in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Full disclosure of Prior Period Errors is provided in the Notes to Financial Statements while waiting for the issuance of LGAM for clarification as to this matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly observe the provisions in COA Circular 2013-004 to promote good governance through transparency and accountability in handling government funds and property.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Signboards were required for all projects implemented and photo of the said formed part of the supporting documents in processing of payments.
observations.reason: Complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the officials tasked to closely monitor the status of work accomplishment for the project as to avoid the incurrence of negative slippage of more than 10% which is already a ground for the termination of the contract.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The project was already completed and inspected by the DILG technical team as of December, 2019.
observations.reason: The said project is reported as completed based on the LGU’s last quarter report of government’s projects, programs and activities for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to compute all outstanding RPT/ SET Receivables and update taxpayers’ ledger cards and reconcile the result with the accounting records to arrive at the proper adjustment to be made.
Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of every year thereafter to compute and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for each year for the annual booking of RPT/SET Receivables and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile their records on RPT/SET Receivables.
Enforce the contract for the computerization of real property records and require the contractor to make good their obligations under the contract and impose sanctions for non-performance, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011 The Municipal Treasurer prepared already the certified list of taxpayers at the end of the year since the AOM was served.
observations.reason: Complied accordingly with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly enforce the collection of the Receivable Account amounting to ₱430,000.00.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Demand letters were already sent to the beneficiaries of the Swine Fattening project.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II No. 11 page no. 52-53 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the local chief executive to enforce liquidation of the funds granted in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 and the Memorandum of Agreement governing the grant and implementation of these projects. File appropriate sanctions against the NGOs/POs to ensure compliance with the provisions of the MOA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Partial return of receivables were collected this year.
observations.reason: Partial return was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare a journal entry to reverse the entry made for the liquidation of LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative unless approval from the fund donor/grantor has been secured for the use of the funds for other purpose. The journal entry to be made is Debit Due from NGO/PO Account and Credit Due to NGAS. Submit justification why LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative will not be required to return the money to the municipality to implement the project for its intended purpose. Require the LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative to immediately liquidate the previous financial assistance of ₱1,000,000.00.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The Accountant prepared the entry to the adjusting entry per JEV No. 401-140704 in CY2014 in compliance with the AOM received. Demand was also sent for the liquidation of the said funds released to the cooperative. The LGU already received one million pesos as partial collection.
observations.reason: Effected already adjusting entry but the entire amount was still not fully collected/liquidated.

observations.recommendation: Management should immediately liquidate financial assistance received, once the purposes had been accomplished. Likewise, excess funds received and those that are no longer needed should be returned or transferred as income of the general fund subject to the consent of the grantor.
observations.managementAction: AAR Unexpended funds: A check was made payable to Provincial Treasury but they are asking when the assistance was received by the LGU. But on the previous communication with the Provincial Accountant we can return the money after the reconciliation in the Provincial Accounting Office. This will be done this CY 2020.
observations.reason: Out of the 41 projects of the LGU, only 26 were implemented and 15 were not implemented. The allocated amount which was not implemented during the year totaled ₱15,866,570.80.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer must conduct physical inventory to determine the existence of these animals.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the Municipal Agriculture Officer for the inventory of the asset as there was no proper turnover and monitor if the recipients have implemented the conditions provided in the MOA.
observations.reason: Since it was difficult for the management to account for the said asset as there was no proper turnover of documents due to the sudden death of the former MPDC who handled the said project, the audit team recommended that management submit a written report that the goats or the breeding stocks were no longer existing and could not be properly traced. As of March 31, 2020, the required written justification was still not forwarded to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality, through the Municipal Agriculture Office should look into this to determine the existence of the Kabir chicken, otherwise proper adjustment should be made in the books of accounts. Moreover, proper monitoring should be made to determine the status of the breeding stocks dispersed to the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2008 The list for the distribution of various eggs to another recipients was done and the adjustment of the books had been done.
observations.reason: Since it was difficult for the management to account for the said asset as there was no proper turnover of documents due to the sudden death of the former MPDC who handled the said project, the audit team recommended that management submit a written report that the kabir chickens were no longer existing and could not be properly traced. As of March 31, 2020, the required written justification was still not forwarded to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  Accounting  Unit  of  the
   municipality to compute and record immediately the  depreciation  in  the
   books of accounts in order to present fairly the value of  Infrastructure
   Assets and other affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Health
   Officer to  immediately  comply  and  install  the  Automated  Hematology
   Analyzer to facilitate the inspection by the COA TITS office and for  the
   audit team to ascertain the reasonableness of the amount  paid  to  avoid
   disallowance in audit.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal  Accountant  to
   prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and to take up the
   necessary adjustments in  the  books  of  accounts  to  ensure  that  all
   reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted  on  a  timely  basis  and
   perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error  and
   omissions.

We recommended that  Municipal  Development  Council  optimize  the
   utilization of the 20% Development Fund  and  ensure  that  projects  are
   implemented fully within the time frame.

        For projects that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive  efforts
   should be made to fast track the completion of the same and ensure  close

   Development Plan.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant effect  directly  the  necessary
   correction in the financial statement where  it  occurred  following  the
   requirements provided in IPSAS 3 including such  disclosure  of  certain
   items in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the statutory positions of  LDRRM  Officer  and  LDRRM
   Staff  responsible  for  Operations  and  Warnings  be  filled  up
   immediately  by  the  municipality,  instead  of   designating   existing
   employees to the same and in order to properly  discharge  the  functions

We recommended that management submit the GAD Accomplishment Report

observations.managementAction: The recommendation on the observation for the purchase  for two (2) motorboats was  complied and the required documents  were submitted to COA Technical  Committee for inspection.
Previous years’ collections on fees from the Local Civil Registrar were already transferred to the General Fund Account and all current collections are taken up in the latter account.
The Municipal Engineer submitted the required documents to the Technical Inspection and Technology Services of COA.

A supplemental budget was made for additional appropriations for the wirings of the generator set to the municipal buildings. Program of work was prepared and submitted for bidding. Failure on first bidding happened and only in December, 2018 that the project was awarded to the contractor and started the project in December 27, 2018.

Excess funds from completed projects were returned on time.  90% of the funds downloaded in CY 2017 were completed and funds downloaded in CY 2018 are completed and some are on-going as of December 31, 2018.
The Mayor issued a Memorandum Order instructing the Municipal Accountant or her representative to inspect the project after the receipt of the notice to proceed.
Demand letters were sent to the recipient and partial settlement was made in January, 2019.
The project is 100% completed and the DILG has already inspected the project which then instructed the Engineer and contractor to do some minor revisions and/or corrections.
Demand letters were being sent to the recipients of the projects and legal action against them are being prepared.

Journal Entry for the reversion was already done upon receipt of the Notice of Suspension. The amount was taken up as Due from NGO/POs since CY 2014.  Demand for the settlement has been done.  Partial payment of their previous financial assistance of One Million was done.

Projects downloaded from the province were partially implemented. Previous years’ balances were remitted but not accepted due to the non-availability of the supporting documents on the year the funds were downloaded and who are the grantors from the province.  We had made agreement with the provincial accountant and instructed us to submit again the schedule of all funds from them so they can assist.

List of the outstanding RPT/SEF receivables for CY 2017 was submitted by the municipal treasurer but Accounting has to reconcile the records to the books considering on the difficulty to set up the balances at the end of the year due to manual recording.  PPSAS program will be used in CY 2019.

The Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the Municipal Agriculture Officer for the inventory of the said breeding stocks and monitor if the recipients have implemented the conditions provided in the MOA.

Reversion of entry was done immediately upon receipt of the notice of suspension. Demand letter was sent for the recovery of the funds but the recipient requested to submit another liquidation for the said fund.
The list for the distribution of various eggs to another recipients was done and the adjustment of the books had been done.

observations.recommendation:
Management should:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to compute all outstanding RPT/ SET Receivables and update taxpayers’ ledger cards and reconcile the result with the accounting records to arrive at the proper adjustment to be made.
Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of every year thereafter to compute and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for each year for the annual booking of RPT/SET Receivables and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile their records on RPT/SET Receivables.
Enforce the contract for the computerization of real property records and require the contractor to make good their obligations under the contract and impose sanctions for non-performance, if warranted.

observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to prepare a listing of the tax payers with the corresponding amounts due and collectible.

observations.recommendation: Management should immediately liquidate financial assistance received, once the purposes had been accomplished. Likewise, excess funds received and those that are no longer needed should be returned or transferred as income of the general fund subject to the consent of the grantor.

observations.reason: Excess funds was not summarized and one project was proposed but province need the resolution of the grants to be secured prior to grantor did already not exist.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality, through the Municipal Agriculture Office should look into this to determine the existence of the Kabir chicken, otherwise proper adjustment should be made in the books of accounts. Moreover, proper monitoring should be made to determine the status of the breeding stocks dispersed to the beneficiaries.

observations.reason: Inventory was still on going.

observations.recommendation:
The Bids and Awards Committee should be required to submit a valid
justification on why the procurement was made even without the approved
plans and specification. The Municipal Engineer, Inspectorate Team and the
Project Monitoring Committee should submit a valid justification on the
inspection and acceptance of the project as 100% completed or replacement
of the two units be made to avoid personal liability on their signatures in the
project accomplishment report and disbursement of government funds.

We recommend that the management strictly observe the provisions
in COA Circular 2013-004 to promote good governance through
transparency and accountability in handling government funds and
property.

We recommend for the local chief executive to enforce liquidation of
the funds granted in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2007-
001 and the Memorandum of Agreement governing the grant and
implementation of these projects.
File appropriate sanctions
against the NGOs/POs to ensure compliance with the provisions of the MOA. (AOM No. 2018-003)

We recommend that the municipal engineer immediately furnish the team with a
duly supported response to the said observations and submit the required
documents to facilitate inspection by the TITS office and preparation of the report
on the validity and reasonableness of the amount paid.

We recommend that the municipal engineer immediately comply and
install the Brand New Generator Set to facilitate the inspection and
preparation of the report on the validity and reasonableness of the amount
paid. (AOM No. 2018-005 dated 2/5/2018)

Require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer all fees collected by the Municipal
Local Civil Registrar for petition to correct the day and/or month in the date of
birth or sex deposited under the Trust fund to the General Fund.

We recommend that management require the contractor to immediately rectify the deficiencies noted in the inspection of the projects. The provisions on forfeiture of warranty security, availment of the retention money and other available remedies to recover government funds as provided in Section 62 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 be followed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Fully Implemented The project was re- implemented The contractor had rectified the deficiencies.
observations.reason: The project was re- implemented The contractor had rectified the deficiencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Government endeavor for the immediate implementation of priority projects given by National Government Agencies and other Local Government Units.
For the excess funds from completed projects or projects no longer feasible, we recommend that these should be returned to the source agency in compliance with the guidelines of COA Circular No. 94-013 on the Rules and Regulations in the Grant, Utilization and Liquidation of funds Transferred to Implementing Agencies or request authority from the source agencies to utilize those funds for other priority projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Fully Implemented The projects were implemented
observations.reason: Excess funds from completed projects not yet returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be required to reconcile their records before disposal will be made. Require the accountant to reclassify the unserviceable properties and equipments from their respective Property, Plant and Equipment Account to Other Assets account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Fully Implemented Disposal was already facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal engineer immediately comply and submit the required documents to facilitate review and preparation of the report on the validity and reasonableness of the amount paid.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR Fully Implemented Management already submitted said reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality ensure that the 20% development funds be optimally utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and see to it that the programs projects funded out of the funds are in accordance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum provisions of Joint Circular 1-11 for the programming for development project.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR Fully Implemented The projects were already implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend however, that management make representation with the officials of HDMF on the possible application of the over remittance of employer’s share for succeeding dues on monthly contribution, otherwise the municipal officers and employees are required to refund the excessive remittance to HDMF pursuant to Section 103 of P.D. 1445
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Fully Implemented The deduction of the HDMF was corrected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the barangay treasurer should support the payment to the suppliers with certified copies of the required permits in compliance with National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) Memorandum Circular No. 09-08-91 and Section 4.6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR Fully Implemented Management has submitted the required documents from NTC.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be required to reconcile their records before disposal will be made. Require the accountant to reclassify the unserviceable properties and equipments from their respective Property, Plant and Equipment Account to Other Assets account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality immediately implement the projects/programs/activities funded by the National Government and other agencies in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those PPAs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Fully Implemented The projects were implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer must conduct physical inventory to determine the existence of these animals.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The municipal Inventory is still on going. Treasurer was instructed to coordinate with the Mun. Agriculture Officer on the inventory of the said breeding stocks which was supposedly acquired by the agency sometime in March, 2010 and monitor if the recipients have implemented stipulated conditions in the MOA.
observations.reason: Inventory is still on going

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Government endeavor for the immediate implementation of priority projects given by National Government Agencies and other Local Government Units.
For the excess funds from completed projects or projects no longer feasible, we recommend that these should be returned to the source agency in compliance with the guidelines of COA Circular No. 94-013 on the Rules and Regulations in the Grant, Utilization and Liquidation of funds Transferred to Implementing Agencies or request authority from the source agencies to utilize those funds for other priority projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Excess funds from Unutilized Funds were completed projects not yet returned.
observations.reason: Excess funds from completed projects shall be returned to the source agency, as recommended.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Required Documents submitted were not complete.
observations.reason: Required Documents submitted were not complete. documents were already gathered for submission to the COA

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality strictly enforce the collection of the Receivable Account amounting to P 430,000.00.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Collections were made in the next calendar year
observations.reason: Recipient was not submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the officials tasked to closely monitor the status of work accomplishment for the project as to avoid the incurrence of negative slippage of more than 10% which is already a ground for the termination of the contract.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The project is still on the process of completion due to some problems in the donation of lot for the water source in Anislag & Guimbawian
observations.reason: Negotiation with the lot owner is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare a journal entry to reverse the entry made for the liquidation of LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative unless approval from the fund donor/grantor has been secured for the use of the funds for other purpose. The journal entry to be made is Debit Due from NGO/PO Account and Credit Due to NGAS.

Submit justification why LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative will not be required to return the money to the municipality to implement the project for its intended purpose.

Require the LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative to immediately liquidate the previous financial assistance of P 1,000,000.00.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 A journal entry to reverse for the entry made for the liquidation of LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative was already made. Management also required LAMAC to utilized the funds as per MOA.
observations.reason: A journal entry to reverse for the entry made for the liquidation of LAMAC Multipurpose Cooperative was already made. Management also required LAMAC to utilized the funds as per MOA.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry debiting Construction in Progress- Agency Asset and crediting Other Structures .
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The municipal accountant prepared a journal entry debiting Construction in Progress- Agency Asset and crediting Other Structures
observations.reason: Partial payment was made by the Municipal Engineer. ND No. 13-001101(12) dated Sept. 16, 2013. NFD was issued dated Oct. 10,2014. COE was issued dated Nov. 14, 2014. Partial settlement was made in the amount of P9,200.00.

observations.recommendation: Management should: Require the Municipal Treasurer to compute all outstanding RPT/ SET Receivables and update taxpayers’ ledger cards and reconcile the result with the accounting records to arrive at the proper adjustment to be made. Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of every year thereafter to compute and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for each year for the annual booking of RPT/SET Receivables and require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to periodically reconcile their records on RPT/SET Receivables. Enforce the contract for the computerization of real property records and require the contractor to make good their obligations under the contract and impose sanctions for non-performance, if warranted.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to prepare a listing of the taxpayers with the corresponding amounts due and collectible.

observations.recommendation: Management should grant immediately liquidate financial assistance received, once the purposes had been accomplished. Likewise, excess funds received and those that are no longer needed should be returned or transferred as income of the general fund subject to the consent of the grantor.
observations.reason: Excess funds was not yet returned. Some unutilized funds were already summarized and one project was proposed but the grantor did not exist. A resolution from the grantor is needed prior to approval.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality, through the Municipal Agriculture Office should look into this to determine the existence of the Kabir chicken, otherwise proper adjustment should be made in the books of accounts. Moreover, proper monitoring should be made to determine the status of the breeding stocks dispersed to the beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Inventory was still on the process. MAO had ongoing monitoring on the recipients on KABIR projects and implementation as per MOA.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that disbursements made by the municipality must be completely signed and certified by the Municipal Budget Officer so as to ascertain the existence or the limit of its appropriation in pursuant to the above cited regulation.
We recommend that the municipal engineer immediately comply and submit the required documents to facilitate review and preparation of the report on the validity and reasonableness of the amount paid.

We recommend that the municipality ensure that the 20% development funds be optimally utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and see to it that the programs projects funded out of the funds are in accordance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum provisions of Joint Circular 1-11 for the programming for development project.

The Municipal Engineer and the Project Monitoring Committee should submit a valid justification on the inspection and acceptance of the project as 100% completed and was not implemented in accordance with plans and specification to avoid personal liability on their signatures in the project accomplishment report and misstatement in the financial records.
Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry debiting Construction in Progress- Agency Asset and crediting Other Structures.
Lastly, require the concerned officials to correctly compute the liquidated damages based on the correct accomplishment report and contractor to refund and return to the source agency (DOT) the amount of P288,000.00 equivalent to the 41.18% discrepancy of the project accomplishment and the liquidated damages collected from the contractor.

We recommend that the barangay treasurer should support the payment to the suppliers with certified copies of the required permits in compliance with National Telecommunications Commission(NTC) Memorandum Circular No. 09-08-91 and Section 4.6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer must conduct physical inventory to determine the existence of the said breeding stocks which was supposed to have been acquired by the agency sometime in March, 2010 and monitor if the recipients have implemented stipulated conditions in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: The municipal treasurer was going coordinate said with the Mun. Agriculture Officer on the inventory of the said breeding stocks which was supposed to have been acquired by the agency sometime in March, 2010 and monitor if the recipients have implemented stipulated conditions in the MOA.
observations.reason: Inventory is still on the process. MAO had on going monitoring on the recipients on KABIR projects and implementatio n as per MOA

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the contractor to immediately rectify the deficiencies noted in the inspection of the projects. The provisions on forfeiture of warranty security, availment of the retention money and other available remedies to recover government funds as provided in Section 62 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 be followed.
observations.managementAction: The contractor required to repair the project
observations.reason: Partial payment was made. The repair was already done by the contractor and a re-inspection of the project was already prepared.
